req.title: Lab order routing (boundary)
req.statement: A clinician shall order a lab test; orders shall route to the patient's preferred lab when available. Boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested.
error_pathways: fallback to default lab when preferred unavailable

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-043 covers lab order routing with three distinct test axes: (1) happy-path routing to a patient's preferred lab (EP – valid partition), (2) fallback routing when the preferred lab is unavailable (NEG/DT – error pathway), and (3) boundary values on timestamp and numeric fields such as epoch-zero timestamps and max-integer quantity values (BVA). A fourth test exercises the order-status state machine from 'draft' through 'active' to 'completed' (STC). Together these cover the positive, negative, and boundary mandates of the requirement and trace cleanly to R-HLT-043.

tests.name: test_lab_order_routes_to_preferred_lab
preconditions: Patient record exists with a preferred lab (lab_id='LAB-PREFERRED-01')
Preferred lab is marked available in the lab registry
actions: POST /ServiceRequest with subject referencing the patient and requester referencing the clinician
Include performerType indicating the preferred lab
Capture the returned ServiceRequest resource ID
trace: R-HLT-043: order routed to patient preferred lab when available
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://localhost:8080/fhir"


def clinician_headers():
    return <fixture: get_clinician_auth_headers>


def build_service_request(patient_ref, lab_ref):
    return {
        "resourceType": "ServiceRequest",
        "status": "active",
        "intent": "order",
        "subject": {"reference": patient_ref},
        "requester": {"reference": "Practitioner/CLINICIAN-01"},
        "performer": [{"reference": lab_ref}],
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "2345-7"}]}
    }


def test_lab_order_routes_to_preferred_lab():
    patient_ref = <fixture: preferred_lab_patient_ref>
    lab_ref = "Organization/LAB-PREFERRED-01"
    payload = build_service_request(patient_ref, lab_ref)
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{BASE_URL}/ServiceRequest",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_headers()
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "active"
    performers = body.get("performer", [])
    assert any(p["reference"] == lab_ref for p in performers)
    assert body.get("id", "") != ""

tests.name: test_lab_order_falls_back_to_default_lab_when_preferred_unavailable
preconditions: Patient record exists with preferred lab 'LAB-PREFERRED-OFFLINE'
Preferred lab is marked unavailable in the lab registry
actions: POST /ServiceRequest referencing the patient whose preferred lab is offline
Observe routing decision in the response performer field
expected: Response contains an extension or note indicating preferred lab was unavailable
trace: R-HLT-043: fallback to default lab when preferred unavailable
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://localhost:8080/fhir"
DEFAULT_LAB_REF = "Organization/LAB-DEFAULT-01"
OFFLINE_LAB_REF = "Organization/LAB-PREFERRED-OFFLINE"


def clinician_headers():
    return <fixture: get_clinician_auth_headers>


def test_lab_order_falls_back_to_default_lab_when_preferred_unavailable():
    patient_ref = <fixture: offline_preferred_lab_patient_ref>
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "ServiceRequest",
        "status": "active",
        "intent": "order",
        "subject": {"reference": patient_ref},
        "requester": {"reference": "Practitioner/CLINICIAN-01"},
        "performer": [{"reference": OFFLINE_LAB_REF}],
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "2345-7"}]}
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{BASE_URL}/ServiceRequest",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_headers()
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    performers = [p["reference"] for p in body.get("performer", [])]
    assert DEFAULT_LAB_REF in performers, (
        f"Expected fallback to {DEFAULT_LAB_REF}, got {performers}"
    )
    assert OFFLINE_LAB_REF not in performers
    assert body["status"] == "active"

tests.name: test_lab_order_boundary_epoch_zero_timestamp_and_max_quantity
preconditions: Patient record exists with a preferred lab available
System accepts ISO-8601 timestamps; epoch zero is 1970-01-01T00:00:00Z
Quantity field accepts integers up to system maximum (2147483647)
actions: POST /ServiceRequest with authoredOn set to epoch-zero timestamp '1970-01-01T00:00:00Z'
expected: HTTP 201 or 422 depending on server timestamp validation policy
If 201: response contains authoredOn == '1970-01-01T00:00:00Z' echoed back
If 422: OperationOutcome describes the invalid field clearly
No 500 Internal Server Error under any circumstance
trace: R-HLT-043: boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested
BVA applied to authoredOn (epoch-zero) and quantity (INT_MAX)
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://localhost:8080/fhir"
EPOCH_ZERO = "1970-01-01T00:00:00Z"
INT_MAX_32 = 2147483647
ACCEPTABLE_STATUSES = {201, 422}


def clinician_headers():
    return <fixture: get_clinician_auth_headers>


def test_lab_order_boundary_epoch_zero_timestamp_and_max_quantity():
    patient_ref = <fixture: preferred_lab_patient_ref>
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "ServiceRequest",
        "status": "active",
        "intent": "order",
        "subject": {"reference": patient_ref},
        "requester": {"reference": "Practitioner/CLINICIAN-01"},
        "authoredOn": EPOCH_ZERO,
        "quantity": {"value": INT_MAX_32, "unit": "mL"},
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "2345-7"}]}
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{BASE_URL}/ServiceRequest",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_headers()
    )
    assert resp.status_code != 500, "Server must not crash on boundary inputs"
    assert resp.status_code in ACCEPTABLE_STATUSES
    body = resp.json()
    if resp.status_code == 201:
        assert body.get("authoredOn") == EPOCH_ZERO
        assert body.get("quantity", {}).get("value") == INT_MAX_32
    else:
        assert body.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome"

tests.name: test_lab_order_status_transitions_draft_to_active_to_completed
preconditions: Patient record exists with a preferred lab available
FHIR server supports ServiceRequest status transitions: draft -> active -> completed
actions: POST /ServiceRequest with status='draft' to create the order
PUT /ServiceRequest/{id} with status='active' to activate the order
PUT /ServiceRequest/{id} with status='completed' to complete the order
expected: First PUT returns HTTP 200 and body.status == 'active'
Second PUT returns HTTP 200 and body.status == 'completed'
trace: R-HLT-043: status tracked across order lifecycle
STC: draft -> active -> completed state chain
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://localhost:8080/fhir"


def clinician_headers():
    return <fixture: get_clinician_auth_headers>


def patch_status(resource_id, new_status, current_body):
    current_body["status"] = new_status
    return requests.put(
        f"{BASE_URL}/ServiceRequest/{resource_id}",
        json=current_body,
        headers=clinician_headers()
    )


def test_lab_order_status_transitions_draft_to_active_to_completed():
    patient_ref = <fixture: preferred_lab_patient_ref>
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "ServiceRequest",
        "status": "draft",
        "intent": "order",
        "subject": {"reference": patient_ref},
        "requester": {"reference": "Practitioner/CLINICIAN-01"},
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "2345-7"}]}
    }
    create_resp = requests.post(
        f"{BASE_URL}/ServiceRequest",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_headers()
    )
    assert create_resp.status_code == 201
    body = create_resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "draft"
    rid = body["id"]
    active_resp = patch_status(rid, "active", body)
    assert active_resp.status_code == 200
    assert active_resp.json()["status"] == "active"
    completed_resp = patch_status(rid, "completed", active_resp.json())
    assert completed_resp.status_code == 200
    assert completed_resp.json()["status"] == "completed"
